tagline: An occupied nation becomes a control-war between imperial hierarchy, masked insurgency, and a will-hacking anomaly that turns strategy into governance engineering.
visualizationReason: Code Geass is a control-network story: Geass permissions, command chains, propaganda legitimacy, and betrayal edges continuously rewire who can issue orders and who obeys; node-graph most accurately surfaces that shifting authority topology.

headerFlavor.loreQuote: The only ones who should kill are those prepared to be killed.
headerFlavor.sysWarning: // [COMMAND_OVERRIDE]: CONSENT LAYER BYPASSED — AUTHORITY GRAPH MUTATING IN REAL TIME

powerSystem.name: Geass
powerSystem.subtitle: Contract-Bound Command Exploit
powerSystem.loreDesc: A supernatural power granted through contracts with Code bearers. Each user manifests a personalized ability, and Lelouch's Geass can force absolute obedience once eye contact is established.
powerSystem.systemDesc: A per-user authority override protocol that writes temporary or permanent instructions directly into a target's decision layer. It is high impact but bound by strict trigger conditions and runaway-evolution risk.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: Lelouch accidentally commands Euphemia to kill the Japanese, proving one unstable instruction can cascade into national-scale collapse.

powerSystem.name: Knightmare Frame Warfare
powerSystem.subtitle: Sakuradite-Powered Mobility Supremacy
powerSystem.loreDesc: Humanoid war machines define the military meta. Superior frames like the Lancelot and Guren repeatedly overturn larger conventional forces through speed and specialized systems.
powerSystem.systemDesc: An arms-race platform where sakuradite energy density, maneuver tech, and pilot cognition decide battlefield throughput. Technical edge compounds political edge by rapidly changing territorial control.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: Suzaku's Lancelot breaks the Battle of Narita's momentum by outclassing legacy units and restoring Britannian operational control.

powerSystem.name: Symbols, Masks, and Broadcast Legitimacy
powerSystem.subtitle: Narrative Command Infrastructure
powerSystem.loreDesc: Zero's mask turns one exile prince into a global revolutionary symbol. Public declarations, trials, and spectacle warfare recruit civilians and destabilize imperial narrative control.
powerSystem.systemDesc: A legitimacy engine that converts perception into manpower, diplomatic recognition, and compliance. Information theater functions as strategic middleware between tactical victories and regime durability.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: The Black Rebellion proves Zero can reframe insurgency from terrorism into governance alternative, forcing global actors to re-evaluate allegiance.

characters.name: Lelouch Lamperouge
characters.title: Zero / Disruption Architect
characters.loreBio: An exiled prince who weaponizes Geass, theatrics, and statecraft to collapse Britannia and then stage-manage a global reset.
characters.systemBio: Primary control-graph mutator combining command override, legitimacy capture, and terminal sacrificial planning.
characters.signatureMoment: Executes Zero Requiem and turns his own assassination into a governance reboot.

characters.name: Suzaku Kururugi
characters.title: Knight of Zero / Enforcement Blade
characters.loreBio: Japan's former heir to peace-through-law who becomes Lelouch's executor and the living mask that stabilizes the postwar order.
characters.systemBio: High-trust enforcement process with elite combat throughput; converts political agreements into coercively credible outcomes.
characters.signatureMoment: Kills Emperor Lelouch in public to complete Zero Requiem and absorb perpetual symbolic burden.

characters.name: C.C.
characters.title: Code Bearer / Contract Broker
characters.loreBio: An immortal witness to cycles of abuse who grants Lelouch the King's Power while searching for release from endless continuity.
characters.systemBio: Metaphysical gateway node that provisions Geass permissions and cross-era protocol knowledge.
characters.signatureMoment: Keeps Lelouch operational after network collapse and re-centers the final plan.

characters.name: Stadtfeld, Kallen
characters.title: Black Knights Strike Ace
characters.loreBio: A rebel elite pilot whose dual Japanese/Britannian identity mirrors occupation fracture and insurgent resolve.
characters.systemBio: High-output tactical node translating strategy into hard wins that preserve rebel bargaining power.
characters.signatureMoment: Holds off Britannia's best units during late-war operations with Guren's Radiant Wave armament.

characters.name: Charles zi Britannia
characters.title: Emperor / Ragnarök Architect
characters.loreBio: Britannia's emperor preaches strength while secretly attempting to erase lies and grief by merging all consciousness.
characters.systemBio: Root-admin actor pursuing forced metaphysical unification via C's World overwrite.
characters.signatureMoment: Activates Ragnarök Connection in the Thought Elevator to hard-overwrite human individuality.

characters.name: Schneizel el Britannia
characters.title: Prime Strategist / Damocles Controller
characters.loreBio: A rational imperial strategist who seeks peace through total fear using Damocles and F.L.E.I.J.A. monopoly.
characters.systemBio: Stasis optimizer replacing politics with constant annihilation leverage.
characters.signatureMoment: Uses Damocles broadcasts to enforce compliance through instant planetary punishment threat.

characters.name: Nunnally Lamperouge
characters.title: Moral Anchor / Political Heir
characters.loreBio: Lelouch's sister, repeatedly instrumentalized as a legitimacy symbol but still capable of rejecting strategic cruelty.
characters.systemBio: High-trust consent token that influences public acceptance across rival authority blocs.
characters.signatureMoment: Publicly rejects Lelouch's tyranny narrative and exposes the emotional cost of systemic engineering.

characters.name: Jeremiah Gottwald
characters.title: Orange / Loyal Counterweight
characters.loreBio: A disgraced officer rebuilt into a cybernetic loyalist who becomes one of Lelouch's most reliable guardians.
characters.systemBio: Security-hardening node with anti-Geass cancellation and high survivability under betrayal-heavy conditions.
characters.signatureMoment: Uses Geass Canceller to restore compromised allies and protect final-phase command continuity.

characters.name: Cornelia li Britannia
characters.title: Imperial General / Order Enforcer
characters.loreBio: A ruthless princess-general whose battlefield discipline embodies Britannian military supremacy.
characters.systemBio: Top-tier conventional command node that stabilizes imperial fronts and suppresses insurgent variance.
characters.signatureMoment: Nearly crushes Black Knights offensives before Geass-layer intelligence flips engagements.

characters.name: Euphemia li Britannia
characters.title: Reform Princess / SAZ Catalyst
characters.loreBio: A sincere reformist whose Special Administrative Zone plan briefly opens nonviolent reintegration before tragedy.
characters.systemBio: Rare high-legitimacy bridge node capable of reducing insurgency through policy reconciliation.
characters.signatureMoment: Her massacre under Geass converts a diplomatic solution into irreversible civil escalation.

characters.name: Lloyd Asplund
characters.title: Camelot Engineer
characters.loreBio: An amoral scientist driving Knightmare innovation with little regard for ideological consequences.
characters.systemBio: Military R&D acceleration process that repeatedly shifts the tactical meta through prototype deployment.
characters.signatureMoment: Delivers Lancelot upgrades that repeatedly restore Britannian combat overmatch.

characters.name: Kaname Ohgi
characters.title: Black Knights Political Operator
characters.loreBio: A founding insurgent whose trust crisis during the Black Knights split helps topple Lelouch's command.
characters.systemBio: Mid-tier coalition governance node; susceptible to misinformation and elite manipulation.
characters.signatureMoment: Leads the Black Knights' break from Zero after manipulated evidence of betrayal.

relationships.loreDesc: Friends become opposite methods for justice but converge for final peace engineering.
relationships.systemDesc: Disruption node and legitimacy enforcer form a two-key release protocol.

relationships.loreDesc: C.C. grants Geass and repeatedly stabilizes Lelouch when his identity fragments.
relationships.systemDesc: Exploit user depends on gateway provider for backend access and context.

relationships.loreDesc: Kallen keeps fighting for Zero despite secrecy, doubt, and collateral costs.
relationships.systemDesc: Primary strike executor sustains strategic credibility through wins.

relationships.loreDesc: Everything Lelouch builds is framed as a world for Nunnally.
relationships.systemDesc: Emotional objective variable that can override optimal policy calculations.

relationships.loreDesc: Father and son clash over whether peace requires deleting human contradiction.
relationships.systemDesc: Competing root-governance models: plural agency vs forced unification.

relationships.loreDesc: Two grand strategists propose incompatible final-order architectures.
relationships.systemDesc: Regime-reset optimization competes with fear-stasis optimization.

relationships.loreDesc: Ohgi helps depose Zero after manipulated evidence and trust collapse.
relationships.systemDesc: Coalition node exits commander graph, fracturing insurgent chain-of-command.

relationships.loreDesc: Suzaku's frontline force counters Schneizel's remote-control deterrence doctrine.
relationships.systemDesc: High-mobility strike capability disrupts static command platforms.

relationships.loreDesc: Euphemia is Suzaku's central reform path before catastrophe.
relationships.systemDesc: Legitimacy anchor for Suzaku's inside-the-system strategy.

relationships.loreDesc: Schneizel presents Nunnally as moral face for Damocles governance.
relationships.systemDesc: Civilian-trust node used to normalize coercive deterrence framework.

relationships.loreDesc: Cornelia executes imperial doctrine as one of Charles's strongest commanders.
relationships.systemDesc: Command doctrine inheritance from sovereign node to theater-level enforcer.

relationships.loreDesc: C.C. rejects Charles's attempt to monopolize C's World.
relationships.systemDesc: Legacy protocol holder blocks backend capture by single sovereign actor.

relationships.loreDesc: Jeremiah's devotion gives Lelouch rare dependable protection late-game.
relationships.systemDesc: Security node preserves command continuity under betrayal load.

relationships.loreDesc: Cornelia and Kallen repeatedly collide as imperial order meets insurgent force.
relationships.systemDesc: Top-down armored doctrine clashes with agile insurgent strike node.

relationships.loreDesc: Lloyd develops and tunes Suzaku's Knightmare stack.
relationships.systemDesc: R&D controller increases pilot throughput via continuous upgrades.

relationships.loreDesc: Euphemia's reform legitimacy threatens to outflank Zero's armed path.
relationships.systemDesc: Policy reconciliation node counters insurgency recruitment loop.

factions.name: Holy Britannian Empire
factions.loreDesc: Expansionist monarchy enforcing nobility supremacy and Number-system dehumanization across occupied Areas.
factions.systemDesc: Centralized extraction state optimized for hierarchy, occupation logistics, and coercive propaganda.

factions.name: Order of the Black Knights
factions.loreDesc: Zero-led insurgency that scales from local resistance to global anti-imperial military actor.
factions.systemDesc: Asymmetric coalition converting symbolic legitimacy into distributed force projection.

factions.name: United Federation of Nations
factions.loreDesc: Post-colonial alliance framework built to contain renewed imperial monopoly.
factions.systemDesc: Distributed governance protocol designed to prevent single-node hegemony.

factions.name: Geass Order
factions.loreDesc: Secret institution breeding and managing Geass users for elite designs.
factions.systemDesc: Metaphysical R&D layer controlling exploit acquisition and memory operations.

factions.name: Britannian Military R&D (Camelot/Rakshata Axis)
factions.loreDesc: Competing engineering circles that escalate Knightmare warfare regardless of ideology.
factions.systemDesc: Innovation feedback loop that rapidly mutates combat doctrine and raises collateral risk.

factions.name: Area 11 Civilian Bloc
factions.loreDesc: The occupied population whose fear, hope, and outrage determine which narrative can govern.
factions.systemDesc: Mass-legitimacy substrate; shifts in perceived justice directly alter recruitment and compliance rates.

rules.name: Geass Contracts Demand Precision
rules.subtitle: Power granted through Geass is never free and never perfectly stable.
rules.loreConsequence: Small misreads become catastrophic human consequences when commands override agency.
rules.systemEquivalent: Consent-bypass exploit with strict trigger semantics and escalating failure modes.
rules.loreSubtitle: The king's power grants control, then asks who deserves to wield it.
rules.systemSubtitle: Exploit surface grows faster than operator safeguards.

rules.name: Britannian Hierarchy Converts People into Numbers
rules.subtitle: Empire governance strips occupied identities and ranks human worth by bloodline and utility.
rules.loreConsequence: Dehumanization normalizes routine brutality and fuels insurgent rage.
rules.systemEquivalent: Stratified policy architecture generates high extraction with chronic rebellion feedback.
rules.loreSubtitle: When names are erased, revolt becomes identity recovery.
rules.systemSubtitle: Classification regimes create persistent insurgency fuel.

rules.name: Legitimacy Is a Weapon System
rules.subtitle: Military victories collapse unless a believable governance story follows them.
rules.loreConsequence: Zero survives because people believe he can protect the weak.
rules.systemEquivalent: Narrative control is middleware converting force into durable authority.
rules.loreSubtitle: Masks and broadcasts can recruit faster than rifles.
rules.systemSubtitle: Perception routing multiplies or nullifies battlefield output.

rules.name: Deterrence Without Accountability Becomes Tyranny
rules.subtitle: Damocles-style peace promises order by permanent fear.
rules.loreConsequence: Society freezes under terror until one failure triggers total backlash.
rules.systemEquivalent: Centralized superweapon governance optimizes compliance but destroys adaptive trust.
rules.loreSubtitle: A peace built on annihilation threat is only delayed war.

rules.name: Metaphysical Unity Cannot Be Forced
rules.subtitle: Ragnarök proposes ending pain by erasing individual boundaries.
rules.loreConsequence: Erasing loneliness also erases choice, grief, love, and responsibility.
rules.systemEquivalent: Backend consciousness merge deletes agent diversity and system resilience.
rules.loreSubtitle: A world without lies can also be a world without selves.
rules.systemSubtitle: Homogenization removes variance required for recovery and innovation.

rules.name: Sacrificial Endgames Leave Governance Debt
rules.subtitle: Zero Requiem creates peace through one orchestrated martyr-villain narrative.
rules.loreConsequence: Catharsis buys time but cannot replace institutions forever.
rules.systemEquivalent: Single-event hostility sink reduces conflict temporarily; long-term stability still requires distributed legitimacy repair.
rules.loreSubtitle: Even successful miracles decay without upkeep.
rules.systemSubtitle: Reset events need post-reset governance maintenance.

anomalies.name: Euphemia Command Catastrophe
anomalies.loreDesc: A single accidental command turns reconciliation into massacre and destroys trust in peaceful reintegration.
anomalies.systemDesc: Operator-side failure transforms tactical exploit into civilization-scale legitimacy collapse.

anomalies.name: Ragnarök Connection Attempt
anomalies.loreDesc: Charles and Marianne attempt to merge all consciousness in C's World and erase private selves.
anomalies.systemDesc: Forced backend unification seeks to remove conflict by removing subject-level variance.

anomalies.name: F.L.E.I.J.A. Deployment at Tokyo Settlement
anomalies.loreDesc: Localized superweapon use erases urban space and civilians, shattering prior deterrence assumptions.
anomalies.systemDesc: Instant mass-deletion event invalidates conventional escalation ladders.

anomalies.name: Black Knights Command Fracture
anomalies.loreDesc: Zero's network implodes when trust and information integrity fail simultaneously.
anomalies.systemDesc: Coalition control graph collapses under coordinated disinformation injection.

anomalies.name: Zero Requiem Public Assassination
anomalies.loreDesc: A planned tyrant execution resets global hostility through engineered spectacle.
anomalies.systemDesc: Intentional blame centralization followed by symbolic decapitation triggers short-horizon equilibrium rebound.

anomalies.name: Geass Canceller Restorations
anomalies.loreDesc: Jeremiah restores compromised cognition, undoing prior coercive edits.
anomalies.systemDesc: Counter-exploit process reopens locked decision pathways in controlled subjects.

counterplay.mechanic: Eye-contact overwrite vs immunity windows and cancel tech
counterplay.loreDesc: Control spikes instantly but collapses if target is shielded or command context is malformed.

counterplay.mechanic: Sustained precision dueling vs close-range one-touch kill threat
counterplay.loreDesc: Outcome depends on spacing discipline, energy timing, and prediction cycles.

counterplay.mechanic: Single terror platform vs distributed sabotage and intel leaks
counterplay.loreDesc: Deterrence fails when command confidence and targeting certainty degrade.

counterplay.mechanic: State legitimacy broadcast vs insurgent justice theater
counterplay.loreDesc: Visible imperial cruelty increases rebel conversion and weakens compliance.

counterplay.mechanic: Metaphysical forced unity vs agency-preservation intervention
counterplay.loreDesc: Plural agency survives by destroying overwrite infrastructure.

counterplay.mechanic: Conventional discipline vs engineered battlefield irregularity
counterplay.loreDesc: Superior formation can be broken by precomputed environmental traps.

counterplay.mechanic: Remote-control equilibrium vs close-quarters decapitation strikes
counterplay.loreDesc: Terror governance loses leverage when invulnerable image is punctured publicly.

counterplay.mechanic: Alliance cohesion vs trust poisoning campaigns
counterplay.loreDesc: Coalitions survive only with verifiable intel channels and redundancy in leadership.

causalEvents.name: Britannia conquers Japan as Area 11
causalEvents.consequence: Identity erasure and Number-system rule create long-horizon insurgency pressure.
causalEvents.loreDesc: Occupation trauma seeds the resistance era.
causalEvents.systemDesc: Colonial classification policy creates persistent legitimacy deficit.

causalEvents.name: Lelouch forms Geass contract with C.C.
causalEvents.consequence: A powerless exile gains command exploit capability and births Zero.
causalEvents.loreDesc: The masked rebellion becomes executable, not hypothetical.
causalEvents.systemDesc: Single-node capability upgrade enables asymmetric challenge to empire hardware.

causalEvents.name: Special Administrative Zone massacre
causalEvents.trigger: Uncontrolled Geass command issued to Euphemia
causalEvents.consequence: Reform pathway collapses and total-war logic becomes dominant.
causalEvents.loreDesc: A possible peaceful bridge is drowned in blood.
causalEvents.systemDesc: Trust-network crash pushes all actors toward maximalist strategies.

causalEvents.name: Black Knights depose Zero
causalEvents.trigger: Schneizel and collaborators inject strategic disinformation
causalEvents.consequence: Insurgent command coherence breaks and Lelouch loses coalition custody.
causalEvents.loreDesc: The revolution fractures under suspicion and manipulation.
causalEvents.systemDesc: Information-integrity failure fragments high-centrality rebel graph.

causalEvents.name: Ragnarök Connection destroyed
causalEvents.trigger: Lelouch and allies confront Charles in Thought Elevator
causalEvents.consequence: Forced consciousness merge is prevented; individual agency remains in-system.
causalEvents.loreDesc: Humanity keeps pain and freedom instead of synthetic serenity.
causalEvents.systemDesc: Backend overwrite protocol is terminated before global commit.

causalEvents.name: Zero Requiem executed publicly
causalEvents.trigger: Lelouch seizes empire then stages own assassination
causalEvents.consequence: Global hatred is discharged into a single event and war intensity drops.
causalEvents.loreDesc: The villain dies, and the world gets one chance to breathe.
causalEvents.systemDesc: Engineered hostility sink produces temporary multi-faction equilibrium.

rankings.topTierLore: Actors who can alter nations or consciousness itself.
rankings.topTierSystem: Nodes with both strategic planning capacity and architecture-level override access.

tiers.name: Metaphysical Root Access
tiers.loreDesc: Code bearers and Geass architects who interface with C's World.
tiers.systemDesc: Back-end operators with permissions beyond conventional politics.

tiers.name: Global Command Strategists
tiers.loreDesc: Leaders who shape wars, alliances, and legitimacy narratives.
tiers.systemDesc: High-centrality nodes controlling military and information routing.

tiers.name: Battlefield Execution Elites
tiers.loreDesc: Pilots and officers who convert plans into decisive tactical outcomes.
tiers.systemDesc: Throughput units that realize strategic intent under contested conditions.

tiers.name: Mass Legitimacy Constituency
tiers.loreDesc: Citizens whose belief, fear, or refusal determines whether regimes hold.
tiers.systemDesc: Distributed acceptance layer that ultimately validates or rejects command systems.

aiInsights.casual: Code Geass looks like a mecha rebellion, but the real engine is control. Lelouch doesn't just win fights; he hacks command itself through Geass, propaganda, and political theater. Every major shift comes from changing who people obey: emperors, symbols, superweapons, or a masked revolutionary. The universe feels unstable because military power, public legitimacy, and supernatural coercion are always competing for the same steering wheel.
aiInsights.deep: The universe is a layered authority architecture with three competing stabilization models: imperial hierarchy (Britannia), coercive stasis (Schneizel's Damocles), and sacrificial legitimacy reset (Zero Requiem). Geass acts as a localized consent-bypass exploit that can override individual agency, while Knightmare and sakuradite systems determine hard-force throughput. However, durable governance is decided in the legitimacy layer: symbols, broadcasts, and perceived justice. The decisive mechanic is not raw destruction but control-graph rewiring—who can issue commands, who is trusted to enforce them, and how quickly those trust edges can be collapsed or rebuilt after anomalies like Euphemia's massacre or F.L.E.I.J.A. strikes.

systemQuestions.question: How does Geass create leverage without direct force?
systemQuestions.answer: Geass acts as a precision command exploit; one successful command can reconfigure institutions far beyond the immediate scene.

systemQuestions.question: Why does the system escalate into empire-scale conflict?
systemQuestions.answer: Causal chains show how localized coercion triggers legitimacy crises, faction realignment, and retaliatory governance.

systemQuestions.question: What rules keep Geass from being a simple win button?
systemQuestions.answer: Usage constraints, visibility risk, and counterintelligence pressure impose hard strategic trade-offs.

systemQuestions.question: How do Britannia, the Black Knights, and shadow operators interact?
systemQuestions.answer: The faction and relationship layers reveal overlapping command hierarchies rather than a single two-side war.

systemQuestions.question: What makes Code Geass unique as a system thesis?
systemQuestions.answer: It models politics as an information-control network where narrative legitimacy is as important as military power.

hero.microHook: Track control edges before reading motives.
hero.thesis: Code Geass is a control-network story: Geass permissions, command chains, propaganda legitimacy, and betrayal edges continuously rewire who…

introductionSummary: Code Geass plays out as a control-war between an occupying empire, a masked resistance cell, and one teenager who can issue absolute commands — except every order reshapes the political graph and creates new dependency edges he didn't intend. Lelouch's Geass permissions and the Knightmare frame technology continuously rewire who can give orders and who must obey. The node-graph renderer exposes what the anime's political intrigue hinges on: authority isn't seized once, it's maintained under constant renegotiation.
